title: quercus pubescens subsp. lanuginosa
intro: Quercus pubescens subsp. lanuginosa, also known as the downy white oak, is a subspecies of deciduous tree native to North America, characterized by its grayish-brown bark and lobed leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, making it effective in treating various health issues such as fever, cough, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Quercus pubescens subsp. lanuginosa include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to promote overall well-being.
